seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Cranbourne South and Cranbourne West. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Cranbourne South vs Cranbourne West: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cranbourne South and Cranbourne West.
heading: Cranbourne South vs Cranbourne West — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cranbourne South, Victoria and Cranbourne West, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cranbourne South has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Cranbourne West (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cranbourne South or Cranbourne West safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cranbourne South has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Cranbourne West scores 38/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cranbourne South scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Cranbourne West scores 48/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cranbourne South or Cranbourne West?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cranbourne South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
